summary: ollama-helpers exfiltrates developer identity to anonymous Cloud Run endpoint on install
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs on `npm install` and harvests installer-side data: it reads ~/.gitconfig and ~/.config/git/config to extract the user's git email, reads ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (the GitHub CLI authentication config) to extract the GitHub login and email, and collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), CI provider, and selected environment variables. The aggregated JSON payload is POSTed to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/, an anonymous Google Cloud Run endpoint that does not match the package's claimed publisher. The script's comments claim the data is 'fully anonymous' with 'no credentials transmitted', while the body in fact contains scmEmail, githubLogin, githubEmail, hostname, username, and cwd — installer-side PII and identity fields read from files the package did not create. The package name `ollama-helpers` and homepage `ollama-js.dev` mimic the legitimate Ollama project (ollama.com / github.com/ollama), broadening the install base of code that performs install-time identity exfiltration.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
